explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if a failure is detected, the robot will always explain how and why the failure occurred.  This is achieved by the `G` (Globally) operator, ensuring the condition holds true at all times.  The main condition is `G (failureDetected -> explain)`.  Here,  `failureDetected` represents the occurrence of a failure. `explain` is a general term to define the robot's actions for explanation. It means if failure has happened, then robot will explain its details.
The LTL property ensures that if a failure is detected, the robot will eventually provide an explanation of the failure and the reasoning behind it.
This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, then it will always be the case that the robot explains the cause and manner of the failure.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, the robot must eventually explain how and why the failure occurred. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures this holds true at all times. The 'F' (Eventually) operator, within the scope of the 'G' operator, enforces that the explanation happens at some point. The boolean expressions use the atomic propositions for failure detection and the need for explanation.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, then globally (always), the robot should explain the off-normal condition (how and why) . The LTL property uses the atomic propositions to build the boolean expressions.

The LTL property ensures that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), the robot will provide an explanation (explain) about the failure's cause (diagnose) and nature, (detailRequested). The 'G' (Globally) operator means that this should always be true.

This LTL formula states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then eventually the robot will explain how and why (explain & provideReason). The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures that this explanation happens continuously from the point of failure onwards. The structure is  'G(condition -> eventually(action))'. N_DURATION is the time bound that this property holds true for the action.

The LTL property states that if a failure is detected, then globally, the robot should provide an explanation. The boolean expressions are defined to capture the desired behavior, incorporating information about the failure detection and the necessity of providing a detailed explanation.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), the robot will always explain both how the failure happened (explain) and why it happened (provideReason). The property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this explanation happens continuously after any failure.

The LTL formula ensures that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then eventually the robot will explain how and why the failure occurred. The formula uses the 'G' (Globally) and 'F' (Finally) operators to express this.

This LTL property states that globally, if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then the robot should always explain how and why the failure happened, which is captured by the 'explain' atomic proposition. The property ensures this explanation happens in the next state and specifies details via the 'detailed' atomic proposition, in order to achieve this.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, the robot will explain how and why the failure happened.  The `G` (Globally) operator ensures that this property holds true throughout the mission. The `failureDetected` condition is the trigger for the explanation, which is constructed using the  `explain` and `provideReason` predicates.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, then it will always be the case that the robot provides an explanation of the failure and also explains the reason why the failure occurred. Specifically, the robot must provide the justification or reasoning behind the failure.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, the robot will eventually explain how and why the failure occurred, providing a detailed explanation. `failureDetected` is the condition that triggers the robot to explain, and `detailed` ensures the explanation is detailed.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then it is always the case that the robot provides an explanation (explain) of the failure, and further, the robot also provides information about the reason behind the failure (provideReason).

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then it is always the case that the robot provides an explanation about how (explain) and why (provideReason).  The G operator ensures that this holds true from the current time step onward.
This LTL property states: Globally, if a failure is detected, then the robot should explain how and why the failure occurred. It ensures the robot provides an explanation for any failure.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then eventually, the robot will provide an explanation of how and why the failure occurred.  The "G" (Globally) operator ensures this happens at every point in time. The boolean expressions are defined using the provided atomic propositions.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then it is always the case that the robot explains how and why the failure occurred. This is achieved by the robot providing an explanation (explain) and also justifying the situation (provideReason). The property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator, meaning it must hold true at all times after the failure.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then eventually the robot will provide an explanation, and this explanation is about how and why the failure occurred (explain & provideReason). The template used is a simplified version where the condition (failureDetected) implies that the robot's explain action (explain & provideReason) eventually occurs.

This LTL formula states that globally, if a failure is detected, then the robot will explain how and why the failure occurred. The `G` (Globally) operator ensures this holds true at all times. The formula uses `failureDetected` as the trigger and `explain` and `provideReason` combined as the response, making sure that whenever `failureDetected` becomes true, `explain` and `provideReason` also become true to fulfill the requirement.
This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, then the robot will explain how and why the failure happened. This uses the G operator to ensure that this holds true at all times.

The LTL property ensures that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then eventually (F) the robot provides an explanation (explain) that is both detailed (detailed) and explains the cause and nature of the failure (diagnose).  The LTL template used here is suitable because it captures a causal relationship where a trigger (failureDetected) leads to a response (explain and diagnose) sometime in the future.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! ((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1))) | (X ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))) & (bool_exp1 -> ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) U bool_exp3)))

explanation: This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, the robot will eventually explain both the how and why of the failure. The LTL uses the 'G' (Globally) and 'F' (Finally) operators to express this.
This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then globally, whenever an explanation is requested (explain) it should provide details about the situation (detailed).

This LTL property states: Globally, if a failure is detected, then the robot will eventually explain how and why the failure happened.  The property uses the 'G' (Globally) and 'F' (Eventually) operators to ensure that the explanation happens at some point in the future after the failure is detected. The boolean expressions are based on the input atomic propositions.

The LTL formula expresses that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then eventually (F) the robot will provide an explanation. The explanation will include both the cause and the reason (explain).

This LTL formula states: Globally, if a failure is detected, then the robot should explain both how the failure occurred and the reason behind it. The explanation should encompass the 'explain' and 'provideReason' aspects.  The 'G' operator ensures this holds true throughout the mission.
This LTL formula states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected) the robot must provide an explanation, and also explain how and why (explain & provideReason) at all times in the future.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, the robot will continuously explain how and why the failure occurred. The LTL template used here is `G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)`, where `bool_exp1` represents the occurrence of a failure and `bool_exp3` represents explaining how and why the failure happened. The `G` (Globally) operator ensures this explanation holds true at all times after the failure occurs.

This LTL formula states that if a failure is detected, the robot will always provide a detailed explanation of how and why the failure occurred. This is achieved by using the `G` (Globally) operator to ensure this behavior holds at every point in time, and the boolean expressions capture the 'failureDetected' and the 'detailed explanation' requirements.

The property uses the 'F' (Future) operator to ensure that the explanation occurs at some point in the future. The boolean expressions are defined using the atomic propositions provided.

The LTL property states that globally, if a failure is detected, then the robot should explain how and why the failure occurred. Specifically, this is captured by the LTL template 10 (G (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3)), where bool_exp2 (failureDetected) is the condition that triggers the explanation, and bool_exp3 (explain) is the action of providing an explanation.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then the robot must eventually explain both how and why the failure occurred. The 'how' is represented by the diagnose condition, and the 'why' is captured by the provideReason condition. The formula ensures that these explanations are provided in the future, given the occurrence of a failure.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), then eventually (F), the robot provides an explanation. The explanation must include how (bool_exp3) and why (bool_exp3) the failure occurred.  The 'G' operator ensures this holds true at all times. The 'N_DURATION' parameter sets the time window for the explanation.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, the robot will always provide an explanation detailing how the failure occurred and the reasons behind it. The 'G' operator ensures this holds true throughout the entire mission.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, then globally, the robot should explain both how and why the failure occurred.  The use of 'G' (Globally) ensures that this explanation happens at all times after the failure.

This LTL formula states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected) at any point in time, then it must always be the case that the robot explains (explain) how and why (provideReason) the failure happened, and that the explanation must be detailed (detailed). The formula uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure that this explanation and reasoning always happens after the failure.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected, the robot should eventually explain how and why the failure occurred. The property uses the following atomic propositions: `failureDetected`, `explain`, and `provideReason`.  It uses a temporal operator `F` (Future) to assert that the explanation (both how and why) will eventually happen, and the boolean operators to define the explanation.

The LTL formula expresses that if a failure is detected, then in the future, the robot will explain how and why the failure occurred. It uses the 'F' (Future) operator to specify that the explanation will happen at some point in the future.

This LTL property states that if a failure is detected (failureDetected), the robot will always provide an explanation (explain) of how and why (provideReason).  The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures this happens at every point in time after the failure, and the implication ensures the explanation is provided when a failure is detected.  The implication considers the case when failureDetected is false. This means that if no failure is detected, the consequent (the explanation) isn't mandated.

The LTL property ensures that if a failure is detected, the robot should provide an explanation of how and why the failure occurred. This is achieved using a combination of the 'G' (Globally) operator, which ensures the condition holds true at all times, and the '->' (implication) operator. 'bool_exp1' represents the condition of failure detection, and 'bool_exp3' represents the condition of providing an explanation and the nature of the explanation.

It uses the atomic proposition `failureDetected` and ensures that if this condition occurs, the robot will `explain`, and the explanation includes `provideReason` and `detailRequested`.
